nodes.context: One of the Five Families of New York City, active in garment district, trucking, construction, and narcotics.
East Harlem street gang involved in theft and burglary, operated under protection of Gaetano Reina.
One of the Five Families, led by Carlo Gambino, involved in racketeering and airport control.
One of the Five Families, led by Vito Genovese, involved in narcotics and labor racketeering.
One of the Five Families, led by Joseph Bonanno, involved in a plot to take over the Commission.
One of the Five Families, allied with Bonanno and Magaddino against Gagliano.
Buffalo-based Mafia family led by Stefano Magaddino, allied with Bonanno and Profaci.
One of the Five Families, allied with Bonanno, Magaddino, and Profaci against Gagliano.
Florida-based Mafia family led by Santo Trafficante Jr., involved in narcotics with Lucchese.
Jewish-Italian gangster group that performed contract killings for the Mafia.
Sicilian Mafia organization, offshoot of which is the American Mafia.

edges.context: The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of Gaetano Reina, whose family later became the Lucchese family.
Lucchese and Gambino formed a strong bond through marriage and controlled the Commission and JFK Airport.
Lucchese allied with Genovese and Gambino to gain control of the Commission in 1951.
After Apalachin, Lucchese allied with others to set up Genovese's arrest and imprisonment.
Bonanno plotted to murder Lucchese and other Commission bosses in 1963.
Lucchese controlled a narcotics network with Santo Trafficante Jr. and had a longtime alliance with his father.
The Gagliano family worked through Frankie Carbo, a former Murder, Inc. hitman, to fix boxing matches.
The Lucchese family is an offshoot of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra.
Gambino and Lucchese allied to set up Genovese's elimination after Apalachin.
Bonanno and Profaci were part of an alliance against Gagliano on the Commission.
Bonanno and Magaddino were allied against Gagliano and later Bonanno plotted to kill Magaddino.
Bonanno, Magaddino, Profaci, and Mangano were allied against Gagliano on the Commission.
